summary.intent: Apply Codex pass-4 findings to the DateTimePicker implementation: route segment formatting through the intl backend for localization parity with TimeField/DateField, clear focus and return to trigger when navigating past the last segment, and reset ctx.open in SyncProps when the picker isn't open.
summary.outcome: All 3 pass-4 findings were fixed, tested (138 tests passing), spec synced, all 10 CI gates green, and changes committed/pushed. Codex was re-triggered for pass-5.

repo: StubIntlBackend yields identical en-US output, so localizing through intl_backend produces no snapshot churn in tests — safe to refactor without mass snapshot updates.
format_segment_text must be routed through intl_backend to stay consistent with how TimeField and DateField format segments; previously it used a simplified/direct path.
SyncProps apply must explicitly reset ctx.open when the resolved state is not Open — omitting this leaves stale open state after external prop changes.
FocusNextSegment past the last segment should clear focus and request trigger focus, not silently no-op.
set_segment_value requires a two-phase borrow split to keep Rust borrows disjoint when updating segment text via backend.

code.finding: format_segment_text refactored to accept and use intl_backend parameter; build_date_segments and build_time_segments updated to thread backend through, including localizing placeholders.
FocusNextSegment handler updated: when idx >= last segment, clear focused_segment and emit trigger-focus event instead of wrapping or stopping.
SyncProps apply arm now resets ctx.open to false when the resolved open state is not Open.
New tests added for FocusNextSegment-past-last clears focus and SyncProps open reset, bringing total to 138.
Spec updated to document: backend-routed formatting in set_segment_value and helpers, FocusNextSegment past-last behavior, and SyncProps ctx.open reset.

workflow: Iterative Codex review loop: fix pass-N findings → compile → test → spec sync → full CI → commit/push → re-trigger Codex for pass-(N+1).
xci-fast covers 10 gates and is the gate check before committing; snapshot tests are included and catch unintended formatting changes.
Codex threads are replied to and resolved after each commit to keep review state clean.

friction: format_segment_text had 9 call sites requiring mechanical updates after adding the backend parameter — straightforward but tedious.
Rust borrow checker required a two-phase rewrite of set_segment_value to avoid simultaneous mutable/immutable borrows when formatting segment text through self.
open_items: Codex pass-5 triggered — further findings may exist; this session only addressed pass-4.
No line numbers recorded for the specific edits, making future diffing require re-reading the file.
